zsh.h(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.h(3)

NAME

zsh.h - Zsh shell completion generator.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)
Generate zsh completion script.

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion generator.

Definition in file zsh.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Generate zsh completion script.

Definition at line 211 of file zsh.c.

211                                                          {
212   if (!output) {
213     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
214   }
215 
216   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
217    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
218    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
219    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
220    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
221    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
222    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
223   size_t binary_count = 0;
224   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
225 
226   size_t discovery_count = 0;
227   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
228 
229   fprintf(output, "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
230                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
231                   "\n"
232                   "# Configure completion for ascii-chat\n"
233                   "# Use only _complete (no corrections or approximate matching)\n"
234                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete\n"
235                   "\n"
236                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
237                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
238                   "  local -a indices\n"
239                   "  local output\n"
240                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
241                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
242                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
243                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
244                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
245                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
246                   "}\n"
247                   "\n"
248                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
249                   "  local -a indices\n"
250                   "  local output\n"
251                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
252                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
253                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
254                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
255                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
256                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
257                   "}\n"
258                   "\n"
259                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
260                   "  local -a indices\n"
261                   "  local output\n"
262                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
263                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
264                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
265                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
266                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
267                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
268                   "}\n"
269                   "\n"
270                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
271                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
272 
273   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
274   if (discovery_opts) {
275     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
276       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
277       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
278       fprintf(output, "'\n");
279     }
280   }
281 
282   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
283                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
284                   "}\n"
285                   "\n"
286                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n"
287                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
288                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
289                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
290 
291   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
292   if (binary_opts) {
293     zsh_write_combined_args(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
294   }
295 
296   fprintf(output, "}\n"
297                   "\n"
298                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
299                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
300                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
301                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
302                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
303                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
304                   "    return\n"
305                   "  fi\n"
306                   "\n"
307                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
308                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
309                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
310                   "    server)\n"
311                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
312                   "      return\n"
313                   "      ;;\n"
314                   "    discovery-service)\n"
315                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
316                   "      return\n"
317                   "      ;;\n"
318                   "\n"
319                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
320                   "    client)\n"
321                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
322                   "      return\n"
323                   "      ;;\n"
324                   "    mirror)\n"
325                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
326                   "      return\n"
327                   "      ;;\n"
328                   "    # Default/unknown input - show mode selection only if truly empty, otherwise discovery\n"
329                   "    *)\n"
330                   "      # If words[2] is empty AND we're at position 2, show mode selection\n"
331                   "      # Otherwise (even for partial input like 'disc'), use discovery mode (the default)\n"
332                   "      if [[ -z \"${words[2]}\" && $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
333                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
334                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
335                   "        server_modes=(\n");
336 
337   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
338   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
339   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
340   if (mode_server_descs) {
341     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
342       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
343     }
344     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
345   }
346 
347   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
348                   "        client_modes=(\n");
349 
350   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
351   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
352   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
353   if (mode_client_descs) {
354     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
355       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
356       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
357         continue;
358       }
359       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
360     }
361     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
362   }
363 
364   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
365                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
366                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
367                   "      else\n"
368                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
369                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
370                   "      fi\n"
371                   "      ;;\n"
372                   "  esac\n"
373                   "}\n"
374                   "\n"
375                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
376                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n"
377                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
378                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
379                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
380 
381   /* Server options - grouped by category */
382   size_t server_count = 0;
383   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
384 
385   if (server_opts) {
386     zsh_write_combined_args(output, server_opts, server_count);
387     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
388   }
389 
390   fprintf(output, "}\n"
391                   "\n"
392                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n"
393                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
394                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
395                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
396 
397   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
398   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
399   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
400       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
401 
402   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
403     zsh_write_combined_args(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
404     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
405   }
406 
407   fprintf(output, "}\n"
408                   "\n"
409                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
410                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n"
411                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
412                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
413                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
414 
415   /* Client options - grouped by category */
416   size_t client_count = 0;
417   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
418 
419   if (client_opts) {
420     zsh_write_combined_args(output, client_opts, client_count);
421     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
422   }
423 
424   fprintf(output, "}\n"
425                   "\n"
426                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n"
427                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
428                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
429                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
430 
431   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
432   size_t mirror_count = 0;
433   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
434 
435   if (mirror_opts) {
436     zsh_write_combined_args(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
437     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
438   }
439 
440   fprintf(output, "}\n"
441                   "\n"
442                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
443                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n"
444                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
445                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
446                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
447 
448   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
449   if (discovery_opts) {
450     zsh_write_combined_args(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
451   }
452 
453   fprintf(output, "}\n"
454                   "\n"
455                   "# Register completion functions (must come after definitions)\n"
456                   "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
457                   "compdef _ascii_chat 'build/bin/ascii-chat'\n");
458 
459   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
460   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
461 
462   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
463 }










Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
